title: The Coca-Cola Company (KO) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of The Coca-Cola Company (KO), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: The Coca-Cola Company demonstrates exceptional Moat Durability and highly resilient Competitive Momentum. Based on explicit quantitative SEC disclosures—including FY25 revenue of $47.9B, robust $29.5B gross profit margins (~61.6%), and incredibly low capital intensity (just $1.85B CapEx)—the business enjoys structural advantages few peers can replicate. Despite a notable $6 billion transfer pricing tax fight with the IRS, massive $5.4B advertising spends and robust $15.4B goodwill highlight unparalleled brand defense.

competitiveMomentum.title: Competitive Momentum
competitiveMomentum.summary: Revenue grew from $45.7B (FY24) to $47.9B (FY25), underpinned by dominant scale and a massive $5.4B marketing engine that sustains high pricing realization.

factors.name: Revenue Growth vs. Peers
factors.rationale: KO grew absolute revenue from $45.7B in FY24 to $47.9B in FY25. While competitor PepsiCo sits larger at $93.93B total revenue due to snacks, KO's pure-beverage growth remains robust.

factors.name: Market Share Trajectory
factors.rationale: The company's massive $47.9B absolute beverage revenue indicates a sustained, overwhelmingly large global market share.

factors.name: Pricing Power
factors.rationale: Generating $29.5B in gross profit on $47.9B in revenue equates to an outstanding ~61.6% gross margin, proving excellent ability to price effectively over input costs.

factors.name: Product Velocity
factors.rationale: Supported by an immense $5.4B advertising expense in FY25, driving continued consumer turnover, brand relevance, and off-the-shelf velocity.

moatDurability.title: Moat Durability
moatDurability.summary: Coca-Cola's moat is historically wide, driven by asset-light distribution networks (CapEx <4% of revenue) and vast brand intangibles.

factors.name: Switching Costs
factors.rationale: High absolute revenue ($47.9B) heavily supported by $5.4B in annual marketing spend embeds deep brand preference and habitual consumption over competitors.

factors.name: Network Effects
factors.rationale: The global bottling and retail distribution footprint required to seamlessly move $47.9B in top-line physical products is structurally prohibitive for new entrants to replicate.

factors.name: Regulatory & IP Position
factors.rationale: Supported by $15.4B in goodwill representing deep acquired brand IP and formulas, despite currently navigating a noted '$6 Billion Tax Fight' with the IRS over transfer pricing.

factors.name: Capital Intensity Advantage
factors.rationale: Incredibly asset-light profile with FY25 CapEx of only $1.85B generating $11.3B in operating income, resulting in highly efficient free cash flow conversion.

sentimentCatalyst.title: Sentiment & Catalysts
sentimentCatalyst.summary: News sentiment is highly favorable among income investors, supported by solid core management execution growing operating income efficiently.

factors.name: Earnings Estimate Revisions
factors.rationale: Trailing price action currently sits at $75.55, roughly 8% off its 52-week high of $82.00, suggesting stable but non-accelerating forward estimate momentum.

factors.name: News & Narrative Sentiment
factors.rationale: Excellent media visibility and positive narrative tailwinds, recently featured prominently in the financial press as one of the '3 Best Dividend Growth Stocks to Buy in March'.

factors.name: Management & Capital Allocation
factors.rationale: Management successfully grew operating income from $10.9B (FY24) to $11.3B (FY25) while strictly maintaining capital discipline and >61% gross margins.

keyRisks: A reported $6 billion tax fight with the IRS regarding transfer pricing adds regulatory friction and potential financial liability.
Intense competition from diversified food and beverage conglomerates like PepsiCo ($93.93B total revenue).
Heavy reliance on massive advertising spends ($5.4B in FY25) to maintain brand relevance and product velocity.
keyCatalysts: Strong ongoing narrative sentiment as a top dividend growth stock could continue to attract large-scale income-oriented capital.
Highly efficient capital intensity (just $1.85B CapEx on $47.9B revenue) frees up massive cash for buybacks and acquisitions.
Sustained pricing power over input costs, evident in a tremendous ~61.6% gross margin profile.
methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored KO at 82/100 and Opus at 79/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
